title: Workflow & connectivity
intro: Tooling that connects models to files, repositories, applications, and multi-step workflows.

items.name: Filesystem MCP Server
items.description: MCP server providing controlled access to the local file system.
features: Structured access to directories and files.
Explicit scoping and permission boundaries.

items.name: Git MCP Server
items.description: MCP server that exposes Git repositories as a queryable context for AI agents.
features: Access to repository files and version history.
Supports diff inspection and code navigation.

items.name: Sequential Thinking
items.description: MCP server that provides structured step-by-step reasoning tools for AI agents.
features: Explicit reasoning and planning support.
Enables traceable intermediate steps.

items.name: Slack MCP Server
items.description: MCP server that connects Slack workspaces to AI agents.
features: Retrieval of messages and channel history.
Supports integration with collaboration systems.

items.name: LangGraph
items.description: Graph-based orchestration framework for building stateful LLM applications.
features: Directed graphs for modeling agent and tool interactions.
Persistent state management across steps.

items.name: n8n
items.description: Open-source workflow automation platform enabling visual orchestration of AI components.
features: Visual node-based workflow editor.
Self-hosted or managed deployment options.
items.pricing: Open-source and free to use for self-hosting.

items.name: Apache Airflow
items.description: General-purpose workflow orchestration platform used to schedule and manage AI pipelines.
features: DAG-based scheduling and dependency management.
Strong observability and retry semantics.

items.name: Temporal
items.description: Durable execution engine for long-running workflows used to orchestrate reliable AI-driven processes.
features: Fault-tolerant and stateful workflow execution.
Handling of retries, timeouts, and failures.

items.name: LiteLLM
items.description: Unified SDK and proxy server that provides a consistent interface to interact with many LLM providers.
features: Single API to access multiple LLM providers.
Built-in proxy server for routing and cost tracking.

items.name: Portkey
items.description: Control plane for AI applications that provides a unified API to connect with multiple LLMs.
features: Semantic caching, retries, and fallbacks.
Observability and tracking of tokens and cost across providers.

items.name: Martian
items.description: Dynamic router that automatically sends prompts to the best model based on cost and performance.
features: Model-agnostic routing to optimize for quality or cost.
Continuous evaluation of model performance across tasks.
items.pricing: Usage-based pricing based on routed requests.

items.name: MineContext
items.description: Framework for managing long-term and evolving context in LLM-based systems.
features: Persistent storage of contextual information beyond the prompt.
Relevance-based selection of context for reinjection.

items.name: GSD (Get Shit Done)
items.description: Meta-prompting and context engineering system designed for spec-driven development.
features: Structured planning and execution phases.
Multi-agent orchestration with isolated context windows.

items.name: Pixeltable
items.description: Data infrastructure for AI applications that unifies tabular data and embeddings.
features: Combines structured data and vector embeddings in one storage layer.
Supports versioning and reproducible data pipelines.

items.name: Acontext
items.description: Context storage and retrieval framework focused on long-term memory for AI agents.
features: Explicit write and read APIs for persistent context.
Structured storage with metadata for selective recall.
